displayTitle: The Crank Lies. The Meter Tells the Truth.
kicker: RAD SAFETY // EXPOSURE DEVICE · LABELING + USE

beats.narration: An exposure device lives in truck beds and cages. It must identify itself to whoever finds it. Its use is just as scripted.

payload.title: THE DEVICE MUST SPEAK FOR ITSELF
payload.sub: Five facts on a label. A locked, two-person routine.

beats.narration: 10 CFR 34.20 makes the user keep a durable, legible label carrying five facts. Activity needs its assay date, so anyone can decay-correct.

payload.heading: The five facts on the user label

beats.narration: Hazard markings speak to everyone; custody is absolute. Locked, key out, unless watched — and two qualified people staff every field job.
Guide tube and exposure head are mandatory once the source leaves the shield. Then 34.49: after every shot, survey device and tube first.

payload.heading: After every exposure: 10 CFR 34.49
payload.note: RETRACT · CONFIRM AUTO-LOCK · SURVEY FULL DEVICE + ENTIRE GUIDE TUBE · BEFORE FILM

beats.narration: If the pigtail parts, the crank feels normal and the turns come out right, while the source sits exposed. Only the meter proves shielding.

payload.sub: Unshielded 100-curie Ir-192 reads ~50 R/hr at one meter. Survey every foot.
